title: Fix the broken debounce flooding the search API
description:
# Issue #130 - Search fires one request per keystroke

Typing 'mechanical keyboard' in the search box fires 19 API requests. starter/debounce.js wraps the handler in debounce(fn, 300) but the implementation never cancels the previous timer, so every keystroke still schedules its own call.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- store the timer handle returned by setTimeout
- clearTimeout the previous timer before scheduling a new one
- the wrapped function still receives the original arguments after the delay
optimal_hint: Ideal: keep let timerId in the closure, call clearTimeout(timerId) at the top of the returned function, then timerId = setTimeout(...). Preserving this/args via fn.apply(this, args) is the polished version.
